title: Alchemilla tephroserica
intro: Alchemilla tephroserica, also known as mountain lady's mantle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, burns, and other skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla tephroserica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alchemilla tephroserica is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin care and wound healing applications.
taxonomy: Alchemilla tephroserica is a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Alchemilla, which consists of approximately 200 species of flowering plants. Alchemilla tephroserica is native to the Caucasus and Turkey. The plant is often classified as an alpine species, thriving in rocky and mountainous regions. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of infections and inflammatory conditions.
